channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: NYC man charged with attempted murder after allegedly shoving commuter in path of subway
item.description: NYPD officers responded to an assault in progress and found that a 45-year-old man had been struck by a southbound train after being allegedly shoved onto the subway tracks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds apply for restitution for abuse suffered at Florida reform schools
item.description: Hundreds of people who say they suffered physical or sexual abuse at two state-run reform schools in Florida are in line to receive tens of thousands of dollars in restitution from the state, after Florida lawmakers formally apologized for the horrors they endured as children more than 50 years ago.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: California regulation requires insurance companies to offer coverage in high-risk areas
item.description: California insurers will be required to sell coverage in wildfire-prone regions that have seen an insurer exodus in recent years, state Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Wild video shows moment Lego display collapses onto crowd of kids, adults during NYE balloon drop gone wrong
item.description: Fire officials confirmed that 10 people were hurt when a Lego display fell onto attendees at a family-oriented New Year's Eve event at a Peabody, Massachusetts, arcade on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Son of Rev. Jesse Jackson seeks presidential pardon
item.description: CHICAGO — For more than two years, former congressman Jesse Jackson Jr. has sought a presidential pardon. His father, the civil rights icon, has written President Joe Biden about it and Jackson Jr. has written Hunter Biden on behalf of himself and millions of others.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: Harry Chandler, Navy medic who survived Japan's attack on Pearl Harbor, dies at 103
item.description: A Navy medic who helped pull injured sailors from the oily waters of Pearl Harbor after the 1941 Japanese attack on the naval base has died at the age of 103.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Mcavoy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Here are 5 hot-button laws taking effect in 2025
item.description: States across the country are bracing for new laws set to take effect as early as Wednesday, including some that have stirred controversy.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: PlayStations for guns are offered up in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Dozens of cars lined up outside a New Orleans church parking lot this week as residents flocked to exchange firearms for video game consoles through a city-supported gun buyback initiative meant to improve public safety as Louisiana loosens gun ownership laws.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Multiple people injured in balloon-drop incident at Massachusetts arcade
item.description: Cheers of excitement quickly changed to horror when the netted balloons pulled a large Lego display down onto the crowd below, including multiple children, Storyful reports.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH LIVE: Stream the NYC Times Square New Year’s Eve Ball Drop
item.description: Watch live streaming coverage of the annual Times Square Ball Drop in New York City on New Year’s Eve!
item.dc:creator: Ken Haddad, Local 4

item.title: Visiting the Big Apple? Prepare to pay more if you drive to busiest part of Manhattan
item.description: New York’s plan to charge drivers extra to enter the center of Manhattan is set to to take effect Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The killing of an 18-year-old Ohio woman was solved with DNA technology after 43 years
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — A man who was shot dead last month as authorities attempted to serve him an indictment on federal gun charges has been identified as the killer of an 18-year-old Ohio woman in a case that had gone unsolved for 43 years, police announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FDA testing aged raw cow's milk cheese for bird flu
item.description: Raw, unpasteurized milk is a known vector of bird flu when derived from infected cow herds. It can also contain pathogens such as E. coli, salmonella, and both Streptococcus and Staphylococcus bacteria.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alleged rapist doctor used job recruiting sites to meet victims, Los Angeles police say
item.description: (KTLA) - The Los Angeles Police Department is searching for potential victims of a doctor who allegedly used job recruiting sites to target women to drug and sexually assault them.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Kevin Cramer’s son sentenced to 28 years over chase, fatal car crash
item.description: The son of North Dakota Sen. Kevin Cramer (R) was sentenced Monday to 28 years in prison over a police chase and death of a local sheriff’s deputy. Ian Cramer, 43, was charged with manslaughter last December after he crashed his family’s SUV into a police cruiser, concluding an hourlong chase.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Why you should probably throw away your Christmas tree this week
item.description: With the holiday season now behind us, it might be time to consider losing your Christmas trees, too.
item.dc:creator: Tylor Sorensen, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former CNN anchor Aaron Brown, who helped viewers through the Sept. 11 attacks, has died
item.description: Aaron Brown, a veteran television news anchor whose steady hand helped guide CNN viewers through the unfolding tragedy of the Sept. 11, 2001, attacks, has died. Brown died Sunday of pneumonia in Washington, D.C., where he lived, family spokesperson Molly Levinson said. He was 76.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER and R.J. RICO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Watch the 2025 Rose Parade streaming live
item.description: LA's Very Own KTLA 5 is proud to offer live, nonstop coverage of the 136th Rose Parade presented by Honda on New Year’s Day, Jan. 1, 2025. Our broadcast and streaming coverage begins at 4 a.m. with an encore presentation of the 2024 Tournament of Roses Parade with grand marshal Audra McDonald.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: Move over, Gen Alpha: 2025 marks the start of the Gen Beta era
item.description: If you just found out about Generation Alpha, brace yourself for a new crop of kids set to debut on Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: Sakshi Venkatraman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dunkin’ to debut Sabrina Carpenter-inspired espresso drink
item.description: Sabrina Carpenter and Dunkin have been working late throughout the holiday season. On Monday, the breakfast chain announced that Sabrina’s Brown Sugar Shakin’ Espresso, a nod to the Grammy-nominated artist’s chart-topping hit, “Espresso,” will be coming to menus on New Year’s Eve.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Returning to the office? Here are some tips to find flexibility and help navigate the changes
item.description: Heading into 2025, thousands of workers face an unsettling reality: After years of working from the comfort of home, they must return to the office full-time for the first time since the coronavirus pandemic or look for new work.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Bussewitz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jetliner skids off runway and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A jetliner skidded off a runway, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames Sunday in South Korea after its landing gear apparently failed to deploy. All but two of the 181 people aboard were killed in one of the country’s worst aviation disasters, officials said.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: This Utah city is among America's top 5 safest small towns: report
item.description: MoneyGeek, a personal finance site, had researchers analyze last year’s FBI crime data of more than 1,000 small towns and cities where the population is between 30,000 and 100,000.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, ABC 4

item.title: Big Lots reaches deal to keep hundreds of US stores open
item.description: The discount chain Big Lots, which filed for bankruptcy protection in September, has reached a deal that will keep hundreds of its stores and distribution centers open. Big Lots said Friday it will be sold to Gordon Brothers Retail Partners, a firm that specializes in distressed companies.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge signals that contempt hearing for Rudy Giuliani over his assets might not go well for him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge is signaling that Rudy Giuliani’s contempt hearing next Friday might not end so well for the former New York City mayor and onetime personal lawyer for President-elect Donald Trump as two Georgia election poll workers try to collect a $148 million defamation award the
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, WGN 9

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on a Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pennsylvania teacher jailed in Russia formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mexico tests app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: U.S. authorities are obliged to give notice to home-country consulates when a foreign citizen is detained. Mexico says it has beefed up consular staff and legal aid to help migrants in the legal process related to deportation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles building from famous rock album cover goes up in flames
item.description: Once featured on the cover of a Doors album, the former site of the Morrison Hotel was destroyed by a fire on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, ABC 4
